clauses.rationale: This clause only identifies the parties and rental unit; none of the candidate rules apply.
The fixed-term dates do not concern or conflict with any of the candidate rules provided.
The clause only states the monthly rent amount and payment date; it does not conflict with any of the provided rules.
The clause requires only a last-month rent deposit equal to one month's rent and applies it to the final rental period, consistent with ON-DEPOSIT-CAP and ON-NO-EXTRA-DEPOSITS.
The clause requires a security and damage deposit, which is expressly prohibited by ON-NO-EXTRA-DEPOSITS.
The clause requires a non-refundable pet deposit, which is expressly prohibited by ON-NO-EXTRA-DEPOSITS.
The clause expressly prohibits all pets and animals in the unit and on the property, violating ON-NO-PET-BAN.
The clause permits only a refundable key deposit and caps it at the actual replacement cost, which is consistent with ON-NO-EXTRA-DEPOSITS.
The clause requires landlord permission for overnight guests and charges a nightly guest fee, both of which are prohibited by ON-NO-GUEST-BAN.

The clause violates ON-ENTRY-NOTICE because it permits entry at any time without prior notice for inspections or maintenance, rather than requiring at least 24 hours’ written notice and a time between 8 a.m. and 8 p.m.
The cleanliness obligation does not conflict with any of the provided candidate rules.

The clause assigns responsibility for damage caused by the tenant or guests but does not require a prohibited damage or security deposit. None of the candidate rules prohibit this allocation of repair responsibility.
The clause imposes a $75 NSF administration charge, exceeding the $20 maximum permitted by ON-NSF-CAP (excluding any separate bank charges).
The clause requires the tenant to provide post-dated cheques, which is prohibited by ON-NO-POSTDATED; such a payment method must be voluntary.

The clause permits rent increases at any time with only 30 days' notice, contrary to ON-RENT-INCREASE-NOTICE, which requires at least 90 days' written notice and generally limits increases to once every 12 months.

The clause violates ON-DEPOSIT-INTEREST because it states that no interest will be paid on any deposit, while the landlord must pay annual interest on the rent deposit at the rent increase guideline rate.
The clause merely makes contents insurance optional and does not conflict with any of the candidate rules.
The clause imposes an absolute ban on assignment or subletting, contrary to ON-SUBLET-CONSENT, which permits these with landlord consent and prohibits consent from being unreasonably withheld.
The clause imposes a general repair and maintenance obligation and does not conflict with or trigger any of the candidate rules.
The clause violates ON-VITAL-SERVICES because it permits the landlord to deliberately shut off electricity, heat, or water due to late rent.
The clause permits the landlord to change the locks without giving replacement keys to the tenant, which directly violates ON-LOCK-CHANGE.
The candidate rules do not address smoking restrictions, so none apply to this clause.

The clause only makes the tenant responsible for arranging and paying for electricity directly; it does not authorize the landlord to withhold or deliberately interfere with electricity under ON-VITAL-SERVICES. None of the other candidate rules apply.
This severability clause does not conflict with any of the provided candidate rules.
